procmon.reading_process: System (PID 4) during boot; targeted post-boot DcomLaunch/Power trigger produced no matching reads; direct S1 and scheduled-task S1 follow-ups both failed before yielding usable exact-value artifacts

frida.skip_reason: KERNEL_GUARD: the watchdog pair lives under HKLM\\SYSTEM and stays out of Frida.

ghidra.decompile_snippet: The current-build ntoskrnl.exe pass found exact string xrefs for WatchdogResumeTimeout and WatchdogSleepTimeout. Repo-side PoFxInitPowerManagement pseudocode then ties the pair to the directed power-up and directed power-down watchdog timeout globals.

classification.reason: The watchdog timeout pair now has a clean baseline export, exact current-build ntoskrnl string hits plus Ghidra fallback artifacts, a repo-side PoFx pseudocode lead, a successful reboot-verified boot trace, a host-side ETL registry review, a DcomLaunch attribution package that narrows the svchost lead to the service host group containing Power, and four Procmon-adjacent runtime attempts: a working boot-log review with adjacent Session Manager\Power hits, a shell-safe post-boot DcomLaunch/Power trigger that still returned no exact watchdog reads, a direct S1-specific follow-up that disturbed guest ops without leaving usable in-guest artifacts or wake-history confirmation, and a scheduled-task S1 follow-up that still left no task registration, no usable artifacts, and no fresh sleep/resume signal. That gives the candidate a credible cross-layer evidence base, but there is still no shipped app mapping, no validated non-default profile, and no direct live read of the exact watchdog values themselves.
